document.title: Software Development Plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
productModels: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software Development Plan Rev.0 for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector software development, classification, verification and validation, release, and configuration management.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software development and validation documentation for a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, including FDA level of concern classification, EN 62304 software safety classification, risk management, V&V, release, and configuration management activities.
keyPoints: The report is identified as A-TCF-10-143 Rev.0 and was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Jul/01/2024.
The document defines the purpose as developing software to manipulate and utilize the product function of the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
Software classification is described according to FDA guideline levels of concern: Major, Moderate, and Minor.
Software safety classification is described according to EN 62304 as Class A, Class B, and Class C.
The software development process follows Classic waterfall methodology.
Verification and validation are confirmed through software testing, including unit test, integration, and system test.
Software release requires verification completion, residual abnormal condition documentation and assessment, release version documentation, and controlled delivery procedures.
Configuration changes should be recorded and validated before application to the software.

concepts.name: FDA software level of concern
concepts.evidence: S/W is classified into Major, Moderate and Minor by its level of concern

concepts.name: EN 62304 software safety class
concepts.evidence: The software safety classes shall initially be assigned based on concern as follows: -. Class A: There is no injury nor health damage. -. Class B: The possible harm is non-serious injury. -. Class C: The possible harm is death or serious injury.

concepts.name: Classic waterfall methodology
concepts.evidence: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology"

concepts.name: Software verification and validation
concepts.evidence: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

concepts.name: Software risk management
concepts.evidence: As for the software risk management, personnel who participated in the program development process shall decide, analyze potential risk, reason, probability and severity of the software.

concepts.name: Software release controls
concepts.evidence: It should be ensured that the software verification is completed and that the results are evaluated before releasing the software.

concepts.name: Software configuration management
concepts.evidence: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

concepts.name: Wireless cybersecurity controls
concepts.evidence: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network.

evidence.claim: The document is Software Development Plan Rev.0 for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Development Plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Report No.: A-TCF-10-143 (Rev.0)
evidence.pageTarget: Software Development Plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The document date is Jul/01/2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Song, Jinsun Jul/01/2024 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dongho Jul/01/2024 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Jul/01/2024
evidence.pageTarget: Software Development Plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was initially released on 2024.07.01.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision history Rev. 0 Revision date 2024.07.01 The reason & content for establishment and amendment Initial Release
evidence.pageTarget: Software Development Plan for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The purpose is to develop software that can manipulate and utilize product function for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: To use the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector developed, produced and sold by this company according to intention, it aims at developing software that can manipulate and utilize the product function.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Software

evidence.claim: Software is classified under FDA guideline levels of concern as Major, Moderate, and Minor.
evidence.evidenceText: S/W is classified into Major, Moderate and Minor by its level of concern

evidence.claim: EN 62304 software safety classes are Class A, Class B, and Class C based on possible harm.
evidence.evidenceText: The software safety classes shall initially be assigned based on concern as follows: -. Class A: There is no injury nor health damage. -. Class B: The possible harm is non-serious injury. -. Class C: The possible harm is death or serious injury.

evidence.claim: Until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements apply for each software system.
evidence.evidenceText: For each software system, until a software safety class is assigned, Class C requirements applied.

evidence.claim: The software development process follows Classic waterfall methodology.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process of H&abyz Co., Ltd. follows “Classic waterfall methodology", as illustrated in the following chart.

evidence.claim: The development schedule includes analysis, planning, design, implementation, qualification, maintenance, software release, and development plan update stages from 2024.07.01 through 2024.11.25.
Hardware development resources identify the MCU model as Intel Cyclone V.
evidence.evidenceText: Hardware resources Section Resource MCU Function 32Bit Microprocessor MCU Model Intel Cyclone V
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Detector Software Development Environment

evidence.claim: Software development resources include Windows 11 64bit and Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional.
evidence.evidenceText: Software resources Section Resource Operation system Windows 11 64bit Compiler Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional / aarch64-poky-linux-gcc / C# Debugging tool Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional Editor tool Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 Professional
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Detector Software Development Environment

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements include Wi-Fi login ID/password access limitation and encryption such as WPA and WPA2.
evidence.evidenceText: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network. Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Verification and validation are confirmed through unit, integration, and system testing.
evidence.evidenceText: Software verification and validation shall be confirmed through the software test. The software test shall conduct the unit test, integration and system test.

evidence.claim: Software risk management includes deciding and analyzing potential risk, reason, probability, and severity.
evidence.evidenceText: As for the software risk management, personnel who participated in the program development process shall decide, analyze potential risk, reason, probability and severity of the software.

evidence.claim: If software failure could cause a hazard, the probability of failure is assumed to be 100 percent under EN 62304:2006/AC:2008 clause 4.3 a).
evidence.evidenceText: If the HAZARD could arise from a failure of the SOFTWARE SYSTEM to behave as specified, the probability of such failure shall be assumed to be 100 percent [∵EN 62304:2006/AC:2008, Cl. 4.3 a)].

evidence.claim: Before software release, verification completion and evaluation results must be ensured.
evidence.evidenceText: It should be ensured that the software verification is completed and that the results are evaluated before releasing the software.

evidence.claim: All identified residual abnormalities should be documented and evaluated for unacceptable risk.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual abnormalities should be documented. In order to ensure that all identified residual abnormalities are not causing unacceptable risks, It should be ensured that such abnormal conditions have been evaluated.

evidence.claim: The deployed software product version should be documented.
evidence.evidenceText: The version of the software product being deployed should be documented.

evidence.claim: Configuration changes should be recorded in the Software Validation Report and validated before software application.
evidence.evidenceText: In case the software configuration is changed, it should be recorded, managed on 12. Software Configuration Management of the Software Validation Report. In case the software configuration is changed, it should be validated before it is applied to the software.

evidence.claim: Software issues must be recorded, corrective measures conducted, and software used only after required validation is completed.
evidence.evidenceText: Any issues identified with the software shall be recorded and applicable corrective measures shall be conducted. In addition, when correcting the issues of software, the company shall examine the issues, notify them to relevant personnel, and analyze any trend of issues. The software shall be used only after the required validation has completed.

reviewFlags.reason: Specific FDA guidance document title, revision, or date is not identified; source only states FDA Guide Line.
Specific product model numbers are not present in the source text; only B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is identified.
Software release version for the deployed product is not provided; document only requires the releasing version to be documented.
Several clauses contain '[If applicable]' conditions, so applicability to the specific software system is not fully confirmed by the source text.
